data.snippet: Brazil, which is reaping a record soy crop this year, may increase exports of the oilseed to China in 2026 amid lower Argentine shipments and in spite of stronger competition from U.S. farmers, an analyst at Hedgepoint Global Markets said on Thursday.

image.text: A view of soybeans stored in a warehouse of the Cotrijal agricultural cooperative during a record soybean harvest season in Brazil's southernmost state in Nao Me Toque, state of Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil April 3, 2024.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Brazil's soybean exports are expected to fall 3% in 2026 from last year, agribusiness consultancy Safras & Mercado said on Monday, estimating shipments from the world's largest producer and exporter to total 105 million metric tons.
relatedHeadlines.text: Brazil's soybean exports to fall 3% in 2026, says Safras

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Brazil, the world's largest soybean producer and exporter, will reap a record of nearly 178 million metric tons in the 2025/26 marketing year, crop agency Conab said on Thursday, as average yields rise and harvesting is well underway.
relatedHeadlines.text: Brazil soy farmers reaping record crop, Conab confirms

data.text: Brazil to raise soy sales to China after record shipments in 2025, consultancy says

data.snippet: U.S. soybean futures rallied on Thursday to their highest since mid-2024 on hopes for more U.S. biofuel demand and Chinese imports before the market eased in a profit-taking retreat.

image.text: Soybeans are loaded on a truck after being harvested, in San Andres de Giles, on the outskirts of Buenos Aires, Argentina, May 12, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Euronext wheat eased on Wednesday, moving back from a three-month peak under pressure from weakness in Chicago futures and a rise in the euro, traders said.
relatedHeadlines.text: EU wheat lower as euro and Chicago weigh

relatedHeadlines.snippet: European Union soft wheat exports since the start of the 2025/26 season in July had reached 15.38 million metric tons by February 22, compared to 15.11 million the previous week and up 10% from a year earlier, European Commission data showed on Tuesday.
relatedHeadlines.text: EU 2025/26 soft wheat exports up 10% by February 22

relatedHeadlines.snippet: European Union soybean imports for the 2025/26 season, which began in July, had reached 8.11 million metric tons by February 22, down 11% from the same period a year earlier, according to data published by the European Commission on Tuesday.
relatedHeadlines.text: EU 2025/26 soybean imports down 11% by Feb 22, rapeseed down 36%

data.text: GRAINS-Soybeans ease on profit taking after hitting 20-month peak on demand hopes

data.snippet: Ukraine's grain deliveries to its Black Sea ports for export have risen by 2% so far in February compared with January, but volumes remain below February 2025 levels, state railway Ukrzaliznytsia said on Thursday.

image.text: A cargo vessel leaves a port in the Black Sea, amid Russia's attack on Ukraine, in Odesa, Ukraine March 26, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Indicative export prices for Ukraine's 2026 rapeseed harvest have risen to $530-$540 per ton CPT July-August delivery from $510-$520 reported a month earlier, as weather problems could reduce the harvest, farmers' union UAC said on Wednesday.
relatedHeadlines.text: Ukrainian export prices for new rapeseed crop rise amid fears of smaller output, union says

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Record low temperatures across most of Ukraine in February led to deep soil freezing, which could delay the start of the grain sowing campaign, the head of the agricultural department of the state meteorological centre was quoted as saying late on Monday.
relatedHeadlines.text: Ukraine grain sowing may be delayed by deeply frozen soil, chief forecaster says

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Ukraine's wheat harvest may grow by 2.9% to 23.1 million metric tons in 2026 due to an increase in sowing acreage and despite a decline in yield, the UCAB agricultural lobby and the economy ministry said over the weekend.
relatedHeadlines.text: Ukraine sees higher wheat, corn harvest and exports in 2025/26

data.text: Ukraine's grain deliveries to ports rise in February, railway says

data.snippet: Ivory Coast plans to bring forward the start of its cocoa mid-crop season for the first time ever, allowing the regulator to sharply reduce its set price paid to farmers in hopes of boosting sales, two government sources and two regulator sources told Reuters.

image.text: Kouassi Kouassi Frederic, a cocoa farmer, holds cocoa pods at a farm in the village of Remikro in Fengolo, Ivory Coast, February 11, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: The producers of half the world's cocoa - Ivory Coast and Ghana - have struggled to sell beans and pay farmers this year due to ample global harvests, lower cocoa prices and falling demand from chocolate makers for the ingredient.
relatedHeadlines.text: EXPLAINER-How did Ivory Coast and Ghana's cocoa sales crisis come about?

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Ivory Coast will have accumulated about 200,000 metric tons of unsold cocoa by end-March when its main crop concludes unless the government cuts state-regulated farmer prices in order to unlock sales from farmers to traders, industry experts and global trading executives said.
relatedHeadlines.text: Ivory Coast unsold cocoa stocks set to soar if price standoff persists

data.text: EXCLUSIVE-Ivory Coast to cut farmer cocoa price with earlier start of mid-crop, sources say

data.snippet: India is likely to record one of its warmest Marches on record, with above-average temperatures forecast in key wheat and rapeseed-growing states, potentially cutting yields, two weather bureau sources said on Thursday.

image.text: A farmer works in a wheat field near Jaipur, India, February 24, 2023.

data.text: India braces for unusually hot March; wheat, rapeseed crops at risk, sources say

data.snippet: Swiss-based seeds and agrochemicals company Syngenta Group plans to apply in the second quarter for an up to $10 billion listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and has hired banks to manage the IPO, two people with knowledge of the matter told Reuters.

image.text: A cutout in a shape of China's map is seen at Syngenta Group China's Modern Agriculture Platform (MAP) service centre, during a media tour in Wei county of Handan, Hebei province June 11, 2021.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: U.S. agrichemicals company Corteva said on Wednesday it expects the previously announced planned separation into two listed companies to occur sometime in the fourth quarter of this year.
relatedHeadlines.text: Corteva targets fourth quarter for planned split into two companies

data.text: Syngenta plans to kick off process in second quarter for up to $10 billion Hong Kong IPO, sources say

data.snippet: London cocoa futures fell on Thursday, slipping back towards a near three-year low they had touched earlier this week, while coffee prices also declined.

image.text: Cocoa beans are sun-dried at a home in Zambolee, Central Region, Ghana February 10, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Brazilian coffee exports in the 2026/27 season could hit an all-time high of close to 47 million 60-kilogram bags, driven by greater supply of arabica beans due to a bumper crop, Hedgepoint Global Markets said in a report published on Wednesday.
relatedHeadlines.text: Brazil 2026/27 coffee exports could hit record of close to 47 mln 60-kg, Hedgepoint says

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Ghana’s licensed cocoa buyers owe banks as much as $750 million, the association that represents them said, leaving the financial sector starved of cash for bean purchases at a time when lenders are straining to recover from the country's deepest economic crisis in a generation.
relatedHeadlines.text: Ghana’s cocoa buyers owe banks up to $750 million, raising fresh liquidity risks
RUBBER-Japan futures gain for seventh straight session on Asian stocks rally, firm oil

data.text: SOFTS-London cocoa prices slip back towards recent lows

data.snippet: Singapore-listed Wilmar International on Thursday missed estimates for annual core net profit and warned that operating conditions in 2026 will remain challenging as geopolitical tensions and trade tariffs reshape global supply chains.

image.text: Workers stand near palm oil fruits inside a palm oil factory in Sepang, outside Kuala Lumpur, February 18, 2014.

data.text: Wilmar posts profit miss, warns 2026 outlook clouded by tariff tensions

data.snippet: Oil major Shell, the healthier joint-venture partner in Brazilian sugar and ethanol producer Raizen, is ready to pour more resources into a recapitalization of the distressed company, three people familiar with the matter told Reuters.

image.text: Fuel tanks stand inside a Raizen Distribution Terminal in Sao Paulo, Brazil August 20, 2025.

data.text: Shell ready for bigger backstop of Brazil sugar-ethanol JV Raizen, sources say

data.snippet: Prices of rice exported from major hubs in Asia were largely stagnant this week, as demand lagged and ample supplies weighed on the market.

image.text: A worker uses a plastic sheet to cover sacks of rice at a wholesale grain market in the northern Indian city of Chandigarh February 9, 2012.

data.text: ASIA RICE-Export prices stagnant on muted demand, robust supply

data.snippet: Vietnam's domestic coffee prices rose on Thursday as trading activities resumed after a week-long Lunar New Year holiday, while premiums in Indonesia eased with farmers expecting a mini harvest to take place in April or after the Eid al-Fitr festival.

image.text: A trader holds coffee cherries on his hands at a coffee farm in Vietnam's central highland of Da Lat city December 11, 2011.

data.text: ASIA COFFEE-Vietnam domestic prices pick up post-holiday, Indonesia premiums fall

data.snippet: Saudi Arabia has issued a tender to purchase 655,000 metric tons of wheat, the Saudi state grains buying agency said on Thursday. The delivery of the wheat is scheduled for the period from May through July, the General Food Security Authority said.

image.text: A wheat crop ready for harvest are seen on the Canadian prairies near Taber, Alberta, September 7, 2011.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Turkish grain board TMO has provisionally bought about 350,000 metric tons of animal feed corn in an international tender on Thursday, European traders said.
relatedHeadlines.text: Turkey provisionally buys 350,000 tons corn in tender, traders say

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Leading South Korean animal feed maker Nonghyup Feed Inc. (NOFI) bought an estimated 135,000 metric tons of animal feed corn in an international tender on Thursday, European traders said.
relatedHeadlines.text: South Korea’s NOFI buys estimated 135,000 tons corn in tender, traders say

relatedHeadlines.snippet: South Korea's Major Feedmill Group (MFG) has issued an international tender to purchase up to 210,000 metric tons of animal feed corn, European traders said on Thursday.
relatedHeadlines.text: South Korea’s MFG tenders for up to 210,000 tons of corn, traders say

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Jordan's state grains buyer has issued an international tender to purchase up to 120,000 metric tons of animal feed barley, European traders said on Thursday.
relatedHeadlines.text: Jordan tenders to buy 120,000 metric tons feed barley, traders say

data.text: Saudi Arabia issues tender to buy 655,000 metric tons of wheat

data.snippet: Malaysian palm oil futures fell on Thursday, weighed down by weakness in Dalian palm oil and Chicago soy oil prices and sluggish exports, while a firm ringgit also added pressure.

image.text: Fresh oil palm fruit bunches from Melati Hanjalipan cooperative palm oil plantation are collected in Hanjalipan village, East Kotawaringin, Central Kalimantan province, Indonesia, July 22, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.snippet: Nepal's soybean oil exports to India jumped more than tenfold in 2025 to a record high, boosted by duty-free access that helped the Himalayan nation double total exports to its neighbour, industry officials told Reuters.
relatedHeadlines.text: Nepal's soyoil exports to India jump tenfold on duty free access

relatedHeadlines.snippet: ICE canola futures rose for a fourth straight session on Monday, with strength in Chicago soyoil providing support.
relatedHeadlines.text: ICE canola futures rise for fourth session

data.text: VEGOILS-Palm tracks Dalian palm, Chicago soy oils lower; weak exports weigh

data.snippet: More than 30 Japanese men clad in loincloths braved cold and heavy rain in a harvest festival more than two centuries old, held in a small, muddy field in a residential pocket outside Tokyo, the capital.

image.text: Participants in loincloths toss and scatter mud in a mud field during Warabi Hadaka Matsuri (Warabi Naked Festival) in Yotsukaido, Chiba Prefecture, Japan, February 25, 2026.

data.text: 'Blessed rain': Japanese men in loincloths  wrestle in harvest rite
